title: The Pigeon Drop
location_hint: concealed near a shaded outdoor resting spot, mid-trail
theme: KGB intercepted a coded message about a shipment of biryani spices arriving by pigeon

content_brief.tone_notes: Dry, bureaucratic spy-agency humour with a South Asian culinary undercurrent. The KGB voice should feel officious but lightly absurd — think classified memo meets aunty's kitchen. Difficulty 3 means the narrative can include a layer of misdirection or a cipher flavour; the finder has had to work for this. Terrain 2 is easy walking, so no physical-hardship references in the story.

series_context.series_note: This is the second cache in a 3-part series. The content MUST inform the finder that a third and final drop exists and that the full recipe cannot be reconstructed without it. Do not reveal the location of cache 3 — only confirm its existence and that KGB-HQ is monitoring.

must_include: A KGB intercepted dispatch — formatted as a partially redacted classified memo — referencing the coded pigeon message
Mention of 'Khala's recipe' as the intelligence asset being protected or pursued
The biryani spice shipment framed as a high-value covert cargo (e.g., whole cardamom, saffron, or a sealed envelope of 'the blend')
An explicit teaser that a third cache exists and the finder must locate it to complete the mission
A field-agent sign-off instructing the finder to log the find and rehide the cache exactly as found
must_avoid: Naming any real courier service, restaurant, or public landmark
Revealing or hinting at the physical location of KGB-005 or any future cache
Overloading the narrative with too many character names — keep it to one or two KGB agent aliases maximum
Any content that spoils or references the solution to KGB-003
